repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §1.2 Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis has staggered cell-weight/basis results, not four-estimator IF equivalence; eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 is closest but under-specified and not subsuming

published_prior_art.cite: borusyak_jaravel_spiess_2024_restud, sun_abraham_2021_joe, callaway_santanna_2021_joe, wooldridge_2023_ectj: estimator-specific identification/equivalence results, no joint four-estimator iff IF frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 had a raw Gamma/Sigma variance-framing but not this BJS-orthogonal contrast-covariance formula

published_prior_art.cite: borusyak_jaravel_spiess_2024_restud, roth_santanna_2021, chen_santanna_xie_2025: efficiency/EIF benchmarks, not a four-estimator contrast-gap identity

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The variance-gap RHS uses an inverse covariance norm V_Delta,m^+ on C_m tau, while V_Delta,m is described as the covariance of the scalar IF-difference; the proposal must state the score-coordinate covariance convention or replace it with the variance of the projected IF difference.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 lists loadings for BJS, SA, CS, and W but does not display the estimator-specific normal equations tying those loadings to the named published estimators, so the theorem is currently algebraic rather than a verified estimator witness.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite Conjecture 2 with an explicit residual-score vector S_i and covariance K_m so the variance gap is stated as Var((C_m tau)'S_i) or an explicitly whitened equivalent.
Add one compact table in Exhibit 9.1 giving Z_m, Q_m, S_m, and L_m for BJS, SA, CS, and W before listing R_m; this would make Theorem 1 a real estimator witness.
Add a short corollary showing when the C tau diagnostic reduces to a known BJS/Sun-Abraham/Callaway-Sant'Anna special equivalence case.

recommended_next_step: Revise the variance metric and make the finite witness derive the four loadings from named estimator normal equations.
